new_feedback.element: Adapter provenance metadata line (e.g. "fraud_adapter@7c1d · validated 2026-06-19 · owner: risk-eng") in the target-adapter cards, all three states (lines 92, 100, 278, 283, 428, 433)
new_feedback.issue: Set at font-size:10.5px. Prior iteration only lifted the cost-meter metadata to >=12px; this provenance/ownership line carries the audit-relevant facts a procurement reviewer reads most closely (who validated the adapter, when, who owns it) yet renders below the 12px floor. At 10.5px it strains on a 24-inch monitor at modest brightness and is the smallest body text on the screen.
new_feedback.change: Raise this provenance metadata to >=12px to match the cost-meter fix. Provenance/ownership text is the wrong place to shrink type for a procurement audience.

new_feedback.element: Sealed-spec provenance strip ("sha256: 9f2a4c7b… · v3.2.1 · author: j.okafor · created 2026-06-21 14:02Z · prior approved spec"), happy state (lines 129-132)
new_feedback.issue: Set at font-size:10.5px. This strip carries the exact provenance chain (hash, version, author, timestamp, link to the prior approved spec) that establishes the chain of custody a procurement reviewer must verify. It is below the 12px floor and was not covered by the prior cost-meter raise.
new_feedback.change: Raise the provenance strip to >=12px. Hash and authorship provenance is precisely the content that must survive a squint test, not the content to set smallest.
